framework: UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Teachers (AI-CFT) 2024
design_principles: Competencies are inferred from observable learner performance, not worksheet identity.
A single item may provide primary and supporting competency evidence.
Evidence accumulates across worksheets; no single worksheet proves mastery.
Mappings include rationale, strength ceiling, and evidence type for each competency.

competency_levels.Acquire: Identify, recall, recognize, explain basic concepts.
competency_levels.Deepen: Apply, evaluate, compare, select, justify, interpret, integrate, optimize.
competency_levels.Create: Design, adapt, customize, innovate AI-supported solutions.

LO3.1.1.title: Basic AI techniques and applications — conceptual knowledge
LO3.1.1.scope: Foundational vocabulary and concepts only: object, feature, label, dataset, decision, threshold, basic ML terminology. Recall and explain — not tool operation.
LO3.1.1.unesco_reference: Aspect 3, Acquire — foundational conceptual knowledge of AI systems.
primary_evidence: DT Section E (sensitivity/MCR formulas as conceptual metrics)

LO3.1.2.title: How AI works — foundational understanding
LO3.1.2.scope: Explains what decision trees do, why data is needed, and distinguishes AI from non-AI approaches at a conceptual level.
LO3.1.2.unesco_reference: Aspect 3, Acquire — understanding of how AI systems work.
primary_evidence: WS11 Q9, Q10, Q12 (conceptual explanation items)

LO3.1.3.title: Validated AI tool selection
LO3.1.3.scope: Locates, validates, and selects appropriate AI tools (e.g. CODAP, Arbor, EMIT) for an educational data-analysis task.
LO3.1.3.unesco_reference: Aspect 3, Acquire — locate and evaluate AI tools for educational use.
primary_evidence: DT Section B (tool selection and setup)

LO3.2.1.title: Evaluate → select → apply AI tools in educational context
LO3.2.1.scope: Demonstrates evaluate → select → apply cycle: choose variables, build multiple models/trees, compare performance — not threshold arithmetic alone.
LO3.2.1.unesco_reference: Aspect 3, Deepen — operate and apply AI tools in educational contexts.
primary_evidence: DT Section B (three variables, three trees, performance comparison)

LO3.2.2.title: Application skills — model behaviour and threshold reasoning
LO3.2.2.scope: Apply, interpret, compare, justify: threshold application and optimization, decision-tree construction/representation, rule articulation, model behaviour.
LO3.2.2.unesco_reference: Aspect 3, Deepen — Application Skills: explain model behaviour, interpret results, evaluate parameters, select appropriate approaches.

LO3.2.3.title: Problem solving — iterative performance improvement
LO3.2.3.scope: Real problem solving with iterative refinement: optimize thresholds, build multi-level trees, evaluate on test data, improve performance.
LO3.2.3.unesco_reference: Aspect 3, Deepen — apply transferable knowledge to solve real problems.
primary_evidence: DT Sections C–F (optimization, two-level tree, evaluation, test data)

LO3.3.1.title: Create — adapt AI-supported solutions
LO3.3.1.scope: Early indicators of designing or adapting AI-supported educational solutions.
LO3.3.1.unesco_reference: Aspect 3, Create — design and adapt AI-supported solutions.

WS11_Q11.section: Q11 — decision tree construction step ordering
WS11_Q11.guidance: Step 1 is pre-filled on the worksheet. Scored sub-items test procedural understanding of the DT workflow (LO3.1.2), not vocabulary recall (LO3.1.1).

DT_Section_A.section: Section A — prior beliefs and first CODAP exploration
DT_Section_A.guidance: DT_A_Q1 is uninformed prior belief (portfolio_weight=baseline, excluded from peaks). DT_A_Q2–Q4 involve CODAP-guided interpretation and count toward LO3.2.2.

DT_Section_G.guidance: Reflective items use portfolio_weight=diagnostic; they inform the researcher but do not drive AI-CFT level proposals.

portfolio_aggregation.peak_includes: competencies with portfolio_weight=full and evidence_type not prior_belief

Uninformed prior belief: names predictive feature(s) before CODAP analysis. Baseline diagnostic only — excluded from portfolio peak aggregation.
After CODAP exploration: names a feature and cites an observed data/graph pattern (not intuition alone) — early data-interpretation behaviour.
Links data exploration to how a model might use that feature.
Identifies a feature showing meaningful separation between recommended and not-recommended classes from explored data.
Selects first split variable with explicit data-based justification (graph separation, class distinction) — precursor to model design.
Early evaluate→select step before full three-tree comparison in Section B.
Selects and validates an appropriate AI tool (CODAP, Arbor, or EMIT) for the task.
Builds decision trees with different variables — evaluate → select → apply cycle.
Builds decision trees with different variables — evaluate → select → apply cycle.
Compares performance across three trees/variables — full evaluate-select-apply evidence.
